item.title: HipLocal Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: Operating Applications on Compute Engine
item.description: Service Accounts Instance runs with Service Account —represents the instance’s identity and roles Default Service Account available—has roles/editor role Large attack vector Best practice: create custom Service Account with granular permissions—least privilege principle Deploying to Compute Engine Startup script Supported by both Linux and Windows Define in metadata Or store in Cloud Storage and add metadata key startup-script-url with the value of the GCS URL for the script Instance requires roles/storage.

item.title: Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Developer
item.description: Key Fundamentals To Know GCP Services Cloud IAM Cloud Storage Cloud SQL Cloud Bigtable Cloud Bigtable Best Practices Cloud Firestore Compute Engine Operating Applications on Compute Engine GCP Load Balancing Kubernetes Kubernetes Engine Kubernetes Patterns Pub Sub App Engine Cloud Run Cloud Functions Cloud Endpoints API Gateway Vertex AI Cloud Logging Cloud Monitoring Cloud Debugger Cloud Trace Cloud Security Command Center Microservices The Twelve Factor App Message Buses Spinnaker Case Studies HipLocal Case Study

item.title: Message Buses
item.description: Use Cases Distributing workloads—distribute tasks between workers Asynchronous workflows—e.g. order fulfilment Distributing event notifications—alert other services that something has happened, e.

item.title: Microservices
item.description: Overview Loosely coupled services Each microservice communicates via API contract Highly maintainable—Test driven development, automation Independently deployable

item.title: The Twelve Factor Appp
item.description: Codebase Single codebase, many deployments One-to-one relationship between codebase and app Dependencies Explicitly declare and isolate dependencies
